zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard White於1519年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特希利法朗斯時，他的父親Thomas White29歲，母親Agnes Richards24歲。他於大約1538年與Hellen Kirton在英國薩默塞特可福可納結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1578年5月6日在英國薩默塞特希利法朗斯享年59歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭哈普爾素斯 瓦恩波洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard White于1519年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特希利法朗斯时，他的父亲Thomas White29岁，母亲Agnes Richards24岁。他于大约1538年与Hellen Kirton在英国薩默塞特可福可納结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1578年5月6日在英国薩默塞特希利法朗斯享年59岁，埋葬在英国英格兰哈普爾素斯 瓦恩波洛。

nameMeanings.name: White
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

variants.name: Whitty
meanings: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller by the white hedge or the white (forest) enclosure’, from Middle English whit ‘white’ (Old English hwīt) + heie ‘hedge, enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg and Old French haie) perhaps in reference to whitethorn (hawthorn) hedging. This English name has been recorded in Ireland (Wexford) since the 13th century. It has been Gaelicized as de Faoite, see also White .
nickname from Middle English whit ‘white’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English hwīt + ēage). Compare Blackie 2 and Greeney .
variant of   Witty  , with which this surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Whitson
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Whiteson, literally ‘white son’ or perhaps ‘son of someone named White ’. Occasionally, a variant of Whiston .

variants.name: Whitt
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of   White  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Witt  .

variants.name: McQuitty
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhaoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelicization of English White . See also McWhite .

variants.name: Whitecotton
meanings: Apparently a compound of   White   +   Cotton  , but the history is obscure.

variants.name: McElwain
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Bháin (Irish) or Mac Gille Bháin (Scottish) ‘son of the fair-haired lad’, often translated into English as White or Whyte but otherwise indistinguishable, once Anglicized, from Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain in 2 below. Compare Kilbane .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Beathan’, a personal name representing a diminutive of beatha ‘life’. Compare McBain .

variants.name: McWhite
meanings: Irish: partly translated Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Faoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of English White .

variants.name: Wight
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Middle English wiht, wight ‘agile, nimble, strong, brave’ (Old Norse víg with the Old Norse neuter suffix -t, for which compare the word scant), or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht. Possibly sometimes a nickname from Middle English wight ‘living creature; small portion or thing’, or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht.

English: habitational name from the Isle of Wight. The island is recorded as Vectis (its Latin name) c. 150 AD . Its name derives from a British word, perhaps related to Welsh gwaith ‘turn, course’, meaning ‘place of the division’, in reference to the island's position in the Solent.
English and Scottish: variant of   White  .

variants.name: Whites
meanings: English: variant of White with genitive or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Whitman
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Whytman (Old English Hwītmann, from hwīt ‘white’ + mann ‘man’). Compare White 1 and also Wightman , with which this surname was probably confused.
History: John Whitman settled in Weymouth, MA, c. 1638. The poet Walt Whitman (1819–92) was descended from Joseph Whitman, who had settled in Stratford, CT, from England around 1660.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard White was born in 1519, in Hillfarrance, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas White, was 29 and his mother, Agnes Richards, was 24. He married Hellen Kirton about 1538, in Crewkerne, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 6 May 1578, in Hillfarrance, Somerset, England, at the age of 59, and was buried in South Warnborough, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard White est né en 1519, à Hillfarrance, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas White, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Agnes Richards, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Hellen Kirton environ 1538, à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 6 mai 1578, à Hillfarrance, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à South Warnborough, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard White è nato il nel 1519 nel Hillfarrance, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Thomas White, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Richards, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Hellen Kirton circa 1538, nel Crewkerne, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 6 maggio 1578, nel Hillfarrance, Somerset, England, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a South Warnborough, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.Holland, Reusel-de Mierden, Noord-Braban: オランダノオルド-ブラバント
ja.Somerset, England, United Kingdom: イギリスイギリスソメルセト
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Whiteが1519年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトヒルファランスで生まれた時，父Thomas Whiteは29歳，母Agnes Richardsは24歳であった。約1538年にイギリスソメルセトクレウケルンでHellen Kirtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1578年5月6日にイギリスソメルセトヒルファランスで59歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアソウス ワーンボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.St John The Baptist's Church, Pilton, So: 영국 서머세트 피턴 스트 존 테 바프티스트'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Richard White(이)가 1519년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 힐파랜스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas White(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Richards(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1538년 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에서 Hellen Kirton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1578년 5월 6일 영국 서머세트 힐파랜스에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 소우스 워버루에 묻혔다.

pt.St John The Baptist's Church, Pilton, So: St John The Baptist's Church, Pilton, Somerset, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard White nasceu em 1519, em Hillfarrance, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas White, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Richards, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Hellen Kirton aproximadamente 1538, em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 6 de maio de 1578, em Hillfarrance, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em South Warnborough, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St John The Baptist's Church, Pilton, So: ст джон т баптист'с чурч, пилтон, сомирсет, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard White родился в 1519 г. в местности хиллфарранс, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas White было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Richards, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Hellen Kirton около 1538 г. в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 6 мая 1578 г. в местности хиллфарранс, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности соут варнбороую, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard White nació en 1519, en Hillfarrance, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas White, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Agnes Richards, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Hellen Kirton aproximadamente 1538, en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 6 de mayo de 1578, en Hillfarrance, Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en South Warnborough, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
